Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, And Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law And Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ana L. Molleda, Esquire, of Weltin Law Office (510-251-6060) on behalf of the Plaintiff. The Defendant/moving party was not present.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, And To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted is granted as it seeks verifications. The court will not determine the adequacy of the responses based upon this motion. Counsel must meet and confer regarding any responses that are alleged to be inadequate. The court does not order all objections waived, but advises counsel that only claims of privilege would likely be sustained if appropriately asserted. Plaintiffs are ordered to verify all responses within 30 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the failure to verify the responses was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiffs who have not verified their responses, jointly and severally, in the amount of $500, to be paid within 45 days of notice of this order.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT (612)

MOTION To Compel Production Of Documents, And Compel Responses To Form Interrogs-Employment Law And Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted
